# VER-QUIC-API-0006 - Incursa.Quic Public Loopback Connection Comparison Verification

## Scope

This artifact covers only the bounded side-by-side public comparison slice for loopback connection establishment plus disposal through the approved public facades. It does not claim equivalence with the internal helper baselines, public stream-transfer performance, internet or HTTP/3 performance, interop-runner behavior, or broader feature completeness.

## Requirements Verified

- REQ-QUIC-API-0015

## Verification Method

Focused public API requirement-home execution, BenchmarkDotNet evidence, and SpecTrace/render inspection for the bounded side-by-side public loopback connection comparison.

## Preconditions

- The supported Incursa public loopback path for listener/connect establishment already exists.
- The benchmark project targets the same runtime that exposes `System.Net.Quic` on the current machine.
- The benchmark harness can create matching local certificate and client trust inputs for both stacks.

## Procedure or Approach

- Inspect `SPEC-QUIC-API`, `ARC-QUIC-API-0006`, `WI-QUIC-API-0006`, `VER-QUIC-API-0006`, and `REQUIREMENT-GAPS.md` to confirm the comparison boundary stays at the public-facade connection level.
- Run the focused Incursa public API requirement-home tests for `REQ-QUIC-API-0002` and `REQ-QUIC-API-0004`.
- Run `dotnet test tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests.csproj --no-restore -m:1 --filter "FullyQualifiedName~REQ_QUIC_API_0002|FullyQualifiedName~REQ_QUIC_API_0004"` and preserve the output under `artifacts/benchmark-baseline/20260423-public-api-loopback-connection-comparison/Verification/public-api-guard-tests.log`.
- Run `dotnet run -c Release --project benchmarks/Incursa.Quic.Benchmarks.csproj -- --job Dry --filter "*QuicPublicApiLoopbackBenchmarks*" --noOverwrite --artifacts artifacts/benchmark-baseline/20260423-public-api-loopback-connection-comparison/Dry` and preserve the output under `artifacts/benchmark-baseline/20260423-public-api-loopback-connection-comparison/Dry/benchmark.log`.
- Run `dotnet run -c Release --project benchmarks/Incursa.Quic.Benchmarks.csproj -- --job Short --filter "*QuicPublicApiLoopbackBenchmarks*" --noOverwrite --artifacts artifacts/benchmark-baseline/20260423-public-api-loopback-connection-comparison/Short` and preserve the output under `artifacts/benchmark-baseline/20260423-public-api-loopback-connection-comparison/Short/benchmark.log`.
- Run the repo-local SpecTrace render/check flow for the changed canonical JSON artifacts, then run `git diff --check` and the known-baseline core SpecTrace validation profile.

## Expected Result

The trace shows a benchmark boundary that uses only the public listener and connection facades for both Incursa.Quic and `System.Net.Quic`; focused Incursa loopback guards remain green; BenchmarkDotNet executes the matched connection comparison suite; and unsupported stacks are omitted rather than represented by synthetic results.

## Status

Passed on 2026-04-24. Focused Incursa public-loopback guards for `REQ-QUIC-API-0002` and `REQ-QUIC-API-0004` remain green, and the preserved BenchmarkDotNet Dry plus Short runs executed the matched public-facade connection comparison cleanly. The refreshed local Short rerun recorded about 24.62 ms and 352.38 KB allocated for Incursa.Quic versus about 20.96 ms and 87.91 KB allocated for `System.Net.Quic` on this machine for the bounded loopback connect/accept/dispose workload. The repo-local core SpecTrace profile still fails on the standing canonical backlog; this artifact does not claim a public stream-transfer comparison.
